sourceMovie.title: Home
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Home for the same reason it broke through in 2015: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Tim Johnson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The fantasy and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Elio
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Elio (2025) operates in the same comedy as Home, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: *batteries not included
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: *batteries not included (1987) belongs to the same conversation as Home — both anchor their fantasy in comedy emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Stitch! The Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Home's comedy stayed with you, Stitch! The Movie (2003) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The animation through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Leroy & Stitch
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Home excels in comedy, Leroy & Stitch (2006) doubles down on animation to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Phineas and Ferb the Movie: Candace Against the Universe
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Phineas and Ferb the Movie: Candace Against the Universe (2020) is the natural next step after Home: same comedy DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and animation elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: WALL·E
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Home who respond to its animation will find WALL·E (2008) immediately familiar. Both films share a science-fiction sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Enchanted
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Home fans keep landing on Enchanted (2007) — the fantasy is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Lilo & Stitch
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Lilo & Stitch draws from the same comedy well as Home, channeling science-fiction in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2025 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: An American Tail
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: An American Tail (1986) captures the comedy appeal of Home without simply imitating it. The animation streak running through both films is what puts An American Tail at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Titan A.E.
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The animation appeal of Home — that uplifting edge, the science-fiction — translates cleanly into Titan A.E. (2000). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

faq.question: What should I watch after Home?
faq.answer: Try Elio, *batteries not included, Stitch! The Movie—each shares DNA with Home while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Home?
faq.answer: Home (2015) is best described as Fantasy, Comedy, Animation, Science Fiction, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Home worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
